title: From the Bridge to the Ballot — Selma Anniversary March in Coatesville, March 7
summary: Coatesville marks the 61st anniversary of the Selma to Montgomery March on March 7 at Gateway Park, gathering at 8:30 AM.
description:
Coatesville hosts "From the Bridge to the Ballot 2026" on Saturday, March 7, commemorating the 61st anniversary of the historic Selma to Montgomery March. The community gathers at 8:30 AM at Gateway Park (22 Lincoln Hwy) with the march beginning at 9 AM.

Coatesville has a deep history of community resilience and civic engagement. Events like this bring residents together across generations. If you have older family members marching or attending, make sure they're set up safely at home too — especially in the bathroom, where 80% of senior falls happen.

title: Sound Healing and Meditative Tea at Koselig Nook — March 15
summary: Koselig Nook in Coatesville offers a Sound Healing and Meditative Tea Experience on March 15 at 6 PM.
description:
Koselig Nook, a Coatesville wellness spot, hosts a Sound Healing and Meditative Tea Experience on Sunday, March 15 at 6 PM. The evening combines guided meditation with sound therapy and specialty teas.

Wellness practices like meditation and sound healing are great for stress reduction and body awareness. But awareness of your physical environment matters too — especially in the bathroom. If the tub is slippery or there's nothing to hold onto when standing up, that's a risk worth addressing.

title: 20th Annual Wagontown Chapel Sportsman's Event — March 21
summary: The Wagontown Chapel hosts its 20th Annual Sportsman's Event on March 21 starting at 12:30 PM near Coatesville.
description:
The Wagontown Chapel hosts its 20th Annual Sportsman's Event on Saturday, March 21 starting at 12:30 PM at 469 W Kings Highway, near Coatesville. The event has been a Chester County tradition for two decades, bringing together outdoor enthusiasts from across the region.

Twenty years of tradition is something to celebrate. If you've been living in your Coatesville home for just as long, the bathroom may need an update. Grab bars, non-slip surfaces, and shower seats are affordable upgrades that keep you independent at home.

title: Spring Food Sale — Ukrainian Ethnic Food in Coatesville, March 28
summary: Grab homemade Ukrainian food at the Spring Food Sale on March 28 starting at 8 AM at 392 Charles Street.
description:
The Spring Food Sale at 392 Charles Street in Coatesville features homemade Ukrainian ethnic food on Saturday, March 28 starting at 8 AM. It's a local tradition that draws people from across Chester County for pierogies, stuffed cabbage, and baked goods.

Coatesville's cultural diversity is one of its strengths. Whether you're picking up pierogies on Charles Street or helping an older relative in the neighborhood, take a moment to check their bathroom. A grab bar installation starts at $199 and takes less than an hour.
